# hKask CLI Documentation

**hKask** (‚ÑèKask - "A Minimal Viable Container for Agents") - Command-line interface

## Usage

```bash
kask [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>
```

## Options

- `-v`, `--verbose` ‚Äî Enable verbose output
- `-r`, `--registry <PATH>` ‚Äî Registry database path (default: in-memory)
- `-h`, `--help` ‚Äî Print help
- `-V`, `--version` ‚Äî Print version

## Commands

### `kask chat` ‚Äî Interactive agent chat

```bash
kask chat [AGENT]             # Default: Curator
kask chat -m qwen3:8b        # Start with specific model
kask chat -f input.txt        # Non-interactive (file input)
echo "hello" | kask chat -f - -m qwen3:8b  # Pipe with model
```

Arguments:
- `[AGENT]` ‚Äî Agent to chat with (default: Curator)

Options:
- `-t`, `--template <TEMPLATE>` ‚Äî Template ID to use
- `-m`, `--model <MODEL>` ‚Äî Model for inference (e.g., "qwen3:8b")
- `-f`, `--input <INPUT>` ‚Äî Input file (non-interactive mode)

Slash commands (inside chat):
- `/help` ‚Äî Show categorized help, `/help <cmd>` for details
- `/status` ‚Äî System status (CNS, agent, model, pods)
- `/agent [NAME]` ‚Äî Show or switch agent
- `/agents` ‚Äî List registered agents
- `/model` ‚Äî Show current model
- `/model list` ‚Äî List all available models
- `/model <name>` ‚Äî Switch to a specific model (e.g., `/model qwen3:8b`)
- `/model <query>` ‚Äî Fuzzy search models (e.g., `/model qwen` lists all Qwen models)
- `/pods` ‚Äî List agent pods
- `/templates` ‚Äî List registered templates
- `/ensemble` ‚Äî Multi-agent ensemble (sessions, create, join, send)
- `/escalations` ‚Äî List pending escalations
- `/metacognition` ‚Äî Run metacognition cycle
- `/sovereignty` ‚Äî Show sovereignty status
- `/history` ‚Äî Show session turn history
- `/quit` ‚Äî End session

### `kask template` ‚Äî Template management

```bash
kask template <SUBCOMMAND>
```

Subcommands:
- `list` ‚Äî List all registered templates
  - `-t`, `--type <TYPE>` ‚Äî Filter by template type
- `register` ‚Äî Register a new template
  - `-i`, `--id <ID>` ‚Äî Template ID (e.g., "prompt/selector")
  - `-p`, `--path <PATH>` ‚Äî Template file path
  - `-t`, `--type <TYPE>` ‚Äî Template type (prompt, cognition, process)
  - `-l`, `--lexicon <LEXICON>` ‚Äî Lexicon terms (comma-separated)
  - `-d`, `--description <DESC>` ‚Äî Description
- `get <ID>` ‚Äî Get template details
- `search <TERM>` ‚Äî Search templates by lexicon term

### `kask bot` ‚Äî Bot capability management

```bash
kask bot <SUBCOMMAND>
```

Subcommands:
- `list` ‚Äî List bot capabilities
  - `-b`, `--bot-id <BOT_ID>` ‚Äî Bot WebID

### `kask pod` ‚Äî Agent pod management

```bash
kask pod <SUBCOMMAND>
```

Subcommands:
- `create` ‚Äî Create agent pod from template crate
  - `-t`, `--template <TEMPLATE>` ‚Äî Template crate name
  - `-p`, `--persona <PERSONA>` ‚Äî Agent persona YAML file path
  - `-n`, `--name <NAME>` ‚Äî Pod name (optional, defaults to UUID)
- `activate <POD_ID>` ‚Äî Activate agent pod for A2A communication
- `deactivate <POD_ID>` ‚Äî Deactivate agent pod
- `status <POD_ID>` ‚Äî Show agent pod status
  - `-v`, `--verbose` ‚Äî Show verbose details
- `list` ‚Äî List all agent pods

### `kask mcp` ‚Äî MCP server/tool management

```bash
kask mcp <SUBCOMMAND>
```

Subcommands:
- `list-servers` ‚Äî List MCP servers
- `list-tools` ‚Äî List available tools
- `get-tool <NAME>` ‚Äî Get tool definition

### `kask cns` ‚Äî CNS monitoring

```bash
kask cns <SUBCOMMAND>
```

Subcommands:
- `health` ‚Äî Get CNS health status
- `alerts` ‚Äî Get algedonic alerts
- `variety` ‚Äî Get variety counters

### `kask goal` ‚Äî Goal coordination substrate (OCAP-gated, CNS-observed)

```bash
kask goal <SUBCOMMAND>
```

Subcommands:
- `create <TEXT> [--visibility private|public]` ‚Äî Create a goal you own
- `list [--state <STATE>]` ‚Äî List your goals
- `set-state <ID> <STATE>` ‚Äî Transition a goal (legal transitions only)

### `kask docs` ‚Äî Documentation generation

```bash
kask docs <SUBCOMMAND>
```

Subcommands:
- `openapi` ‚Äî Generate OpenAPI specification (JSON)
  - `-o`, `--output <OUTPUT>` ‚Äî Output file path (default: stdout)
- `cli` ‚Äî Generate CLI help documentation (markdown)
  - `-o`, `--output <OUTPUT>` ‚Äî Output file path (default: stdout)
- `all` ‚Äî Generate all documentation
  - `-o`, `--output <OUTPUT>` ‚Äî Output directory

## Examples

```bash
# Start chat session
kask chat

# Chat with model selection
kask chat -m qwen3:8b

# Non-interactive with model
echo "Summarize this" | kask chat -f - -m qwen3:8b

# Inside chat: switch models
/model              # Show current model
/model list         # List all available models
/model qwen         # Fuzzy search for Qwen models
/model qwen3:8b     # Switch to qwen3:8b

# List all templates
kask template list

# Register a new template
kask template register -i prompt/selector -p templates/selector.j2 -t prompt -l "select,route,dispatch"

# Generate OpenAPI spec
kask docs openapi -o docs/openapi.json

# Generate all documentation
kask docs all -o docs/
```

## Template Types

- `prompt` ‚Äî Prompt templates for LLM interaction
- `cognition` ‚Äî Cognitive processing templates
- `process` ‚Äî Process execution templates
